ending: After Caliban defeats Bao Long by disqualification, he finds Nick in void-induced psychosis beside a mortally wounded custodian. Caliban kills Nick in immediate defense and receives the two altered apple seeds that prove Nick's experiment succeeded. Firion then moves to arrest Caliban. Arthur and Mindy help him evade the enforcers, while Charlotte and Xavier knowingly abandon their sect positions to escape with him. Maria Lopez blocks their route to Lucy and attacks, but Caliban fills the area with dark Qi, prevents Lopez from replenishing her power, and forms a protective shell. Contact with his Qi exposes Lopez to the Infinite Sea and breaks her resistance. Caliban spares her, and the three fugitives leave Firion aboard Lucy. They are injured but alive and head toward the system's habitable world to seek natural treasures needed for bronze focuses. Caliban plants Nick's seeds and permits himself to grieve. Lopez plans to seek him through the wider Dragon's Right Eye, Martha Vesper begins a path of vengeance, and the mysteries of the Infinite Sea and the coordinated void-horde attack remain open.
in_short: Cal survives the massacre at Rufi after an encounter with the void gives him undetectable Qi. Rescued by the sentient ship Lucy, he joins Dragon's Right Eye on Firion and secretly advances while elders, instructors, and compulsory duels threaten his place. He builds friendships with Charlotte, Xavier, Nick, and Arthur, saves children during a suspicious void-horde attack, and forms tin and then copper cores through the Infinite Sea. After defeating Bao Long, Cal kills Nick during Nick's void-induced psychosis and becomes the target of an arrest shaped by Maria Lopez's false vampirism theory. Arthur and Mindy help him flee, and Charlotte and Xavier choose exile beside him. The three survive Lopez's attack when Cal develops a dark defensive shell and overwhelms her with the Infinite Sea. Lucy carries them away toward the system's habitable world to seek bronze focuses. Lopez survives with a transformed belief in Cal, while Nick's sister Martha vows revenge.

recaps.text:
Cal and his older brother Brady are vac welders on Rufi, an isolated refueling station. A visiting cultivator succumbs to void-induced psychosis, drains the crew, and kills Brady. The attacker also empties Cal of Qi, but dies when the stolen energies prove incompatible. Cal is clinically dead for more than four minutes before an unknown power from the void restores him, leaving him the station's only survivor.

A disembodied rescuer treats Cal aboard a ship and guides him through his first deliberate Qi control. She is Lucy, the sentient soul ship LC81535. Lucy admits she initially fled Rufi and returned only after the danger passed, then pledges to help Cal in atonement. Cal discovers that ordinary Qi is painful and unusable to him, while the silent energy beyond the hull answers his reach. The experience nearly draws him into emotional emptiness, but memories of Brady and Rufi lead him to choose a finite life with self-made meaning. He decides to cultivate so he can protect others and understand his power. Lucy departs for the Dueling Stars so Cal can seek human cultivation instruction.

During the journey to Firion, Cal opens a fourth meridian and agrees to hide the nature of his Qi. Dragon's Right Eye accepts him but places him among child beginners under Maria Lopez's scrutiny. He enters a culture where official duels are compulsory and resources follow rank. Xavier immediately challenges and defeats him, triggering a panic attack. Charlotte Velereau helps him recover and explains the outpost's hierarchy, while Arthur confirms that Cal's room marks him as a low-status resident.

Caliban Rex cannot absorb normal Qi, yet he secretly opens more meridians and uses his focus-room allowance to build ties with Charlotte and Xavier. Lucy reveals that Cedric Steathen was her final passenger, and Caliban connects Cedric with Brady's death. Seeking a way to approach the Infinite Sea safely, Caliban learns to anchor himself through his bonds and memories. Lopez privately develops an unsupported theory that he may be reanimated or possessed and begins covert surveillance. Nick Vesper discovers Caliban's corpse-like appearance during an illicit skin-meridian opening. He accepts a focus-room hour for silence and later approaches Caliban, Xavier, and Charlotte at dinner, while the sect remains unaware of how quickly Caliban is advancing.

Caliban brings Charlotte into his plan to conceal his progress and shares spare focus-room time with her, Xavier, and Nick. A void horde then strikes Firion. Responsible for a class of children, Caliban kills a beast with a freezer cart, shelters the group, and carries a gravely wounded child through occupied corridors. His cold, Qi-less appearance lets him pass near the creatures, though it also makes a senior cadet mistake him for an undead threat. The child reaches medical care alive.

After the emergency, Lopez suppresses security evidence showing Caliban's rescue and his strange effect on the beasts, preserving it as leverage. Caliban suspects the attack was coordinated because communications were targeted with unusual precision. He responds by opening three more meridians. The rescued child's parents later give him a flexible sword forged partly from the cart that saved their child. Outside the sect, Caliban defends Arthur from men seeking Arthur's missing sister and warns the criminals that Arthur has sect protection. As Dueling Day approaches, Caliban keeps his true advancement hidden. Charlotte arranges favorable matches for herself, Xavier pursues rapid promotion, and Caliban plans controlled defeats rather than expose his abilities.

Caliban loses all three Dueling Day matches by design, while Charlotte earns promotion and Xavier continues to rise. Nick uses an engineered plant technique but is injured and publicly rejected by his demanding family. Caliban intervenes, deepening his concern for Nick. The four friends then visit Lucy, where her nature and pocket dimension are revealed to them. Nick admits he takes medication for depression and would prefer gardening to cultivation. Lucy openly confirms that she abandoned Caliban at Rufi before returning to save him.

Caliban and his friends fake slower, sect-approved progress while he opens his spine meridian and advances out of basic meditation and cycling. Bao Long, previously known as Instructor Long, blocks his combat advancement and announces Lopez's deadline for completing the beginner courses. Charlotte and Xavier develop a hybrid combat style for Caliban using recordings of Cedric Steathen, while Caliban refuses to leave Firion without Nick. Nick begins trying to adapt living seeds to Caliban's unusual Qi. Caliban then attempts the dangerous muscle-meridian opening in secret. An accident leaves him unconscious and seizing, but his friends summon help and maintain the cover story. His survival and the opening's success remain unresolved as he is taken for surgery.

Caliban survives surgery and learns that his muscle meridian finished opening while he was unconscious. Lopez misreads the medical evidence as support for a theory that he is a vampiric experiment. Caliban opens his final meridian, takes vac-welding work, and spends months training. He forms an unusual tin core by grounding himself in curiosity, agency, friendship, Lucy, and the dead of Rufi. Standard external techniques fail because his Qi disperses, but he soon advances secretly to copper while presenting himself as tin.

Nick's seed experiments repeatedly fail, and his determination to find the Infinite Sea becomes increasingly obsessive. Meanwhile, Victor kidnaps Arthur and tortures him for Clara's location. Caliban rescues Arthur, severs Victor's armed limb, and leaves the extortionist alive. With Lopez's deadline near, Caliban challenges Bao Long. He uses his hidden physical development and Cedric-derived forms until Long loses control, employs illegal Qi restraint, and attempts a killing blow. The official disqualifies Long and awards Caliban victory. Caliban refuses better housing because he will not abandon Nick. Returning to Housing D, he finds Nick with black eyes beside the Qi-drained custodian, shifting the crisis from sect rivalry to an immediate threat.

Nick is suffering void-induced psychosis and has fatally attacked the Housing D custodian. Caliban kills him in immediate defense. Before dying, Nick gives Caliban two altered apple seeds, one carrying luminous Qi and the other dark stillness, proving his research succeeded. Caliban grieves and blames himself for missing Nick's decline. Firion prepares to arrest him, with Bao Long among the enforcers. Arthur and Mindy conceal Caliban's departure from Housing D, and Charlotte and Xavier voluntarily sacrifice their sect standing to flee beside him.

Maria Lopez intercepts the three before they can reach Lucy. She reveals Caliban's identity as Andrew Rex, Brady's surviving brother, and explains the false vampirism theory behind her attempt to capture him. Charlotte and Xavier reject her offer of leniency and fight. Lopez's bronze power badly injures all three, but Caliban floods the area with dark Qi, denying her access to ambient recovery, and creates a close defensive shell. Contact with that Qi exposes Lopez to the Infinite Sea and leaves her surrendered. Caliban spares her and escapes aboard Lucy. Three days later, the group heads toward the system's habitable world for natural treasures needed to pursue bronze. Caliban plants Nick's seeds and grieves without numbing himself. Lopez plans to seek him, while Martha Vesper vows to avenge Nick.
